items.title: French Heat Wave Kills Poultry, Corn Crops in Key Farming Nation
items.description: France’s record-breaking heat has cut as much as 30% of corn production in Europe’s top farming nation and killed hundreds of thousands of poultry.

items.title: Treasuries Slip Before Payrolls as Traders Weigh Warsh Comments
items.description: Treasuries edged lower as traders awaited a US employment print that may give clues on the Federal Reserve’s interest-rate path after Chairman Kevin Warsh struck a dovish tone on inflation.

items.description: China and the US are seeking to roll back tariffs on some agricultural products, as they look to preserve a broader trade truce that was struck last year.

items.title: Brazil Rail Operator Opens New Route to Boost Soymeal Exports
items.description: Brazilian railway operator VLI SA is starting a new export route for soybean meal, addressing key bottlenecks as the country attempts to boost shipments of the animal feed ingredient.

items.title: Morgan Stanley Among Banks Challenging Growing Dollar Optimism

items.description: Swiss inflation slowed in June for the first time in eight months, suggesting the impact of lower oil costs are feeding through to the domestic economy.

items.description: Carlsberg A/S confidentially filed draft papers for an initial public offering of its India unit to raise as much as $700 million, according to people familiar with the matter.
